researchProjects.description: Commander Davis wants to create a radio tower over the summer of 2024. It will require collaboration of the entire camp. When completed, it will greatly improve our chances of survival as it extends our communication to the entire wasteland!
researchProjects.milestoneDescs: {"milestoneDescs":[{"id":"1","text":"Survivors finished work on the Radio Tower Project"}]}
researchProjects.name: Radio Tower

researchProjects.description:
A large monsterâ€”referred to as the Juggernautâ€”is believed to be linked to a group of masked, robed figures, hereby referred to as Cultists, living in the woods. Their staves drip with a substance tied to infection spread. Infection sites appear to worsen in areas connected to them. The creature itself remains unverified.

Studying this group and their connection to these infection sites may help the camp better understand what weâ€™re dealing with.
researchProjects.milestoneDescs:
{"milestoneDescs":[{"id":"1","text":"Survivor Research Log â€” Entry 1 Topic: Unconfirmed Entity â€” â€œJuggernautâ€�

Date of Entry: August 24th, 2024

Summary: Reports have emerged regarding a potential new infected entity, named â€œThe Juggernaut.â€� While no confirmed visual contact has been recorded, patterns have begun to form around its suspected activity.

Key Observations:
- Dr. Petrov: A letter recovered during the raid on the ritual site was addressed to Camp Still Alive. Its contents suggest that Dr. Petrov may hold a leadership role within the group cultists. The letter also included a direct threat, warning that the camp would not remain safe for long.

- Cultists: Multiple sightings indicate the presence of robed, masked individuals in near major infection blooms. These figures carry staves that reportedly leak a viscous, dark fluidâ€” analysis pending (no samples retrieved).

- Environmental Shift: Areas with increased sightings of the cultists show accelerated infection growth.
Reliability Index: Low-to-moderate. All current data is anecdotal. No visual confirmation of the Juggernautâ€™s form or capabilities exists.However, increasing evidence suggests that it poses a real threat.
Further research required. Contributions encouraged.

Actionable Notes:
- Log and document all sightings of cultists.
- Study new infection sites and find a way to dismantle them.
- Learn how the cultists are communicating with each other."},{"id":"2","text":"Survivor Research Log â€” Entry 2 Topic: Infection Sites

Date of Entry:  June 21st, 2025

Objective: Investigate a known infection site, document conditions, and study a physical fragment of the infection if safe.

Outcome: Mission successful. Team returned with photographic evidence and detailed observations. No live sample was collected due to safety concerns.

Key Findings:
1. Heightened Hostility â€“ Zombies near the infection site showed increased aggression and strength. Recommend extreme caution around these sites.
2. Perimeter Manipulation â€“ Team disrupted the outer infection ring using a makeshift bridge, creating a narrow passage through the site. This suggests the infectionâ€™s spread can be physically interrupted, at least temporarily.
3. Containment Conclusion â€“ Infection sites show no signs of natural decay or permanent controllable regression. The team recommends complete destruction of affected areas via explosives to prevent spread.

Further documentation pending. Suggested next step: map infection site perimeters and flag possible demolition zones.

Field Notes: During the infection site sweep, a photograph was captured of a cultist emerging briefly from behind the treeline. The figure moved erraticallyâ€”drifting in and out of viewâ€”but made no direct attempt to engage.

This behavior suggests a level of reverence or surveillance tied to the site itself. The cultistâ€™s presence reinforces the theory that infection zones are of critical importance to their agenda nued efforts to dismantle and disrupt these areas are strongly justified.

Hand Drawn Image of an Infection Site included in the Research Binder.

Actionable Notes:
- Design and prepare a portable explosive device capable of neutralizing localized infection sites without endangering surrounding terrain or survivors."},{"id":"3","text":"Survivor Research Log â€” Entry 3 Topic: The Cultist Cypher

Date of Entry:  June 21st, 2025

Summary: The team discovered five separate symbol sites, each consisting of carved glyphs arranged in deliberate sequences. Upon comparing the markings, survivors were able to decipher the full alphabet.

Key Findings:
- The glyphs appear to correspond to letters in a substitution cypher.
- The presence of these symbols, organized across the five locations, suggests deliberate encoding meant for cultist communication.
- Placement and repetition imply the cultists are leaving behind tools to decodeâ€”or reencodeâ€”future messages.

A reference of the entire Compiled â€œCultist Cypherâ€� containing the full glyph alphabet can be found in the Research Binder. Survivors are encouraged to use this chart to decode future messages or symbols found on objects, walls, or ritual sites.

Note: Team advises cautionâ€”symbols may also serve as markers or triggers for unknown Cultist behavior. Avoid disturbing them."},{"id":"4","text":"Survivor Research Log â€” Entry 4 Topic: The Hag
Date of Entry:  August 23rd, 2025
Summary: The team tracked recent disappearances to a concealed hut deep in the woods. Inside, they discovered ritual tools, potion ingredients, preserved human remains, and a notebook filled with coded script. Decoding the cipher revealed the occupantâ€”a woman referred to only as the hagâ€”was in direct communication with the cultists.
Key Findings:
Human Remains as Trade:The hag actively traded human remains with cultists. She gleefully describes her excitement at receiving twitching limbs.
Transformation Practices: She lured survivors, then dosed them with potions laced with the infection. Victims were then bound and watched until they died. These zombies now guard her hollow and obey her verbal commands.
Notes: The hag is still at large. If spotted, do not engage alone. Report immediately and fall back to safe distance. Her methods involve deception and heavy use of the infectionâ€”assume any food source or item is heavily infected as well."},{"id":"5","text":"Survivor Research Log â€” Entry 5 Topic: Slowing It Down
Date of Entry: August 23rd, 2025
Summary: Three distinct damage sites were discovered during a sweep of the eastern forest perimeter. Each location bears signs of violent impact consistent with previous Juggernaut activity. The findings confirm both the creatureâ€™s immense strength and its use of a sharp-edged weapon or appendage.

Site Observations:
Gash in Tree: A single, deep strike cleaved through a mature oak. The force required suggests the Juggernaut can easily destroy small barricades and launch survivors with minimal effort.
Fallen Tree on Road: Evidence of a struggleâ€”splintered bark, drag marks, and displaced soilâ€”indicates the creature is large and powerful but lacks precision. Its clumsiness may be exploitable.
Dead End Trail: A wide path carved through dense brush and stone. The sweeping damage patterns suggest repeated strikes with a bladed instrument or limb.

Key Findings:
The Juggernaut wields a sharp, heavy weapon or possesses a natural cutting appendage.
Its attacks are devastating and capable of breaching most camp defenses.
Despite its power, the creatureâ€™s movement appears hindered by its size and lack of agility.

Next Steps:
Tactical recommendation: Construct snares or physical traps designed to exploit its clumsiness. Large obstacles (e.g., logs, trenches, spike pits) may slow or redirect its path. It seems possible to design a trap based around these parameters. Continued Downtime Research on this subject is possible."},{"id":"6","text":"Survivor Research Log â€” Entry 6 Topic: Snare Trap Construction
Date of Entry: August 23rd, 2025

Summary: Following analysis of the Juggernautâ€™s damage sites and movement patterns, we have successfully developed a series of snare traps designed to exploit the creatureâ€™s bulk and lack of agility. This marks a critical advancement in our defensive strategy.

Design Overview:
Trap Type: Weighted cable snares with reinforced anchor points.
Trigger Mechanism: Pressure plates calibrated to the Juggernautâ€™s mass.
Deployment Zones: Narrow trails, choke points, and known patrol routes.
Materials Used: Wood, metal, cloth, and tech.
Construction Requirements: 3 trained Tinkerers working in tandem or 1 Master Craftsman, and roughly 10 minutes of labor.

Tactical Insights:
The Juggernautâ€™s clumsy gait and wide swings make it vulnerable to terrain-based traps.
Snares are designed to entangle limbs and destabilize movement, buying precious seconds for escape or counterattack."},{"id":"7","text":"Survivor Research Log â€” Entry 7 Topic: Confusing the Beast
Date of Entry: August 23rd, 2025

Summary: A team of survivors intercepted a wandering cultist. The cultist resisted violently during apprehension, displaying erratic strength and disregard for injury. Amid the struggle, the subject produced a hidden weapon and inflicted a fatal wound upon themselves with deliberate precision. Death followed within seconds.No final words. No hesitation. The act appeared rehearsed. A note was discovered tucked beneath the cultistâ€™s robes encoded in cipher.

Findings:
Cultists may be conditioned to die upon capture.
There may be a temporary way to disrupt the beastâ€™s behavior.

Note: Subjectâ€™s final expression was serene. As if death was expected. Or welcomed.

Next Steps:
The painkiller slurry may be something we could replicate. It seems possible to design a trap based around it. Continued Downtime Research on this subject is possible."},{"id":"8","text":"Survivor Research Log â€” Entry 8 Topic: Construction of Confusion Trap
Date of Entry: August 23rd, 2025

Summary: Following extensive testing on tissue samples we have identified a compound mixture capable of disrupting the Juggernautâ€™s behavioral cohesion. The slurry induces acute confusion, spatial disorientation, and indiscriminate aggression.

Design Overview:
Materials Used: Various Medical Supplies.
Construction Requirements: 3 trained Tinkerers working in tandem or 1 Master Craftsman, and roughly 10 minutes of labor.

Conclusion: This slurry is viable for emergency disruption only. It is not a cure, not a weaponâ€”merely a window. Use it wisely."},{"id":"9","text":"Survivor Research Log â€” Entry 9 Topic: Cultist Devotion
Date of Entry:  October 18th, 2025

Summary: The team was tasked with tracking and capturing a cultist known to frequent infection markers near the outer perimeter. Visual contact was made with a robed figure observing the treetops in silence. The team initiated a coordinated pursuit and successfully restrained the target.

Critical Outcome:
Before any questioning could begin, the cultist murmured an unknown phrase and bit down on a hidden molar capsule. Death followed within momentsâ€”presumed self-administered toxin. The act suggests cultists are trained to die rather than divulge information. A note was recovered on the body written in code.

Key Takeaways:
Cultists prioritize secrecy above survival.
Future capture attempts must include immediate containment of the mouth and hands.

Status: Mission partially successfulâ€”confirmation of protocol. Interrogation failed. Enemy remains elusive."},{"id":"10","text":"Survivor Research Log â€” Entry 10 Topic: The â€œInvincibleâ€œ Juggernaut
Date of Entry:  October 18th, 2025

Document Type: Coded Note â€“ Partial Decryption Complete

Summary: The recovered note, encoded using a modified substitution cipher, revealrbing insights into the cultâ€™s experimentation on Subject 27. The note included information on how the subject had been tested for weakness. Three attempts had weakened the subject's armor, making him partially vulnerable to all types of attacks, flame exposure, extreme explosive force, and electrical shock.

Next Steps:
It seems possible to design traps based around these parameters. Continued Downtime Research on this subject is possible."},{"id":"11","text":"Survivor Research Log â€” Entry 11 Topic: Elemental Juggernaut Traps
Date of Entry:  October 18th, 2025

Subject Reference:
â€œJuggernautâ€� Status: Active â€“ Vulnerabilities Identified

Summary:
Following the successful decryption of a coded note recovered from a fallen cultist, the research team conducted extensive analysis and field simulations to verify the weaknesses described within. These efforts led to the formulation of three specialized traps, each designed to exploit a distinct vulnerability in the Juggernautâ€™s physiology or behavior. Though the entity has yet to be directly encountered, these traps are considered vital assets should containment or confrontation become necessary.

Confirmed Weaknesses & Trap Designs:

Sustained Flame Exposure
Effect: Slows regenerative processes and causes surface degradation.
Trap: Flame circle with overlapping ignition cycles and thermal saturation.

Large-Scale Explosives
Effect: Internal destabilization and temporary loss of locomotion.
Trap: Pressure-triggered seismic charges embedded in terrain.

High-Voltage Electrical Shock
Effect: Induces seizure-like paralysis and sensory disruption.
Trap: Electrified containment grid with pulse generators.


Construction Requirements to be detailed in the coming days.

Next Steps:
It seems possible to refine these trap designs with even more research. There are two potential directions this research could take: alternate recipes for the traps which will lower the material costs and more powerful mechanisms which will increase the potency of the traps. Continued Downtime Research on this subject is possible."},{"id":"12","text":"Survivor Research Log â€” Entry 12 Topic: Construction of Confusion Trap
Date of Entry:  October 18th, 2025

Summary:
Following extensive testing on tissue samples we have identified a compound mixture capable of disrupting the Juggernautâ€™s behavioral cohesion. The slurry induces acute confusion, spatial disorientation, and indiscriminate aggression.

Design Overview:
Materials Used: Various Medical Supplies
Construction Requirements: 3 trained Tinkerers working in tandem or 1 Master Craftsman, and roughly 10 minutes of labor.

Conclusion: This slurry is viable for emergency disruption only. It is not a cure, not a weaponâ€”merely a window. Use it wisely."}]}
researchProjects.name: Understanding the Juggernaut
